facts_disclosed: Thinking about moving to suburbs near parents (undecided)

turns.content: can I ask you something kind of personal well not personal exactly but like... life direction-ish
Of course, go for it. What's on your mind?
so lately I've been going back and forth about where I want to live and it's driving me crazy because I can't make a decision and I keep changing my mind every other day
That's a big decision, and the fact that you're going back and forth usually means there are real pros and cons on both sides. What are the options you're weighing?

ok so my parents and Sofia are out in Cicero right and I keep going back and forth on whether I should just move out there too. like I feel like I'm missing everything being in the city, Sofia is growing up so fast and I only see her on weekends sometimes not even that

That's a really meaningful pull — wanting to be closer to family, especially watching a sibling grow up. Cicero would definitely cut down on the distance. What's holding you back from making the move?

because I LOVE Logan Square. like I love my apartment I love the neighborhood I love being able to walk to restaurants and coffee shops and the blue line is right there. it's where all my friends are and it feels like me you know?? Cicero is... not that lol

That's a real tension — identity and lifestyle versus family proximity. Logan Square has that walkable urban energy that's hard to replicate in the suburbs. How far is the commute from Cicero to your hospital? That's an important practical factor too.

honestly the commute wouldn't be terrible, maybe 30 minutes by car depending on traffic. it might actually be about the same as what I do now from Logan Square by train. and rent would definitely be cheaper which would help with my student loans and everything

So the commute is a wash and you'd save money on rent — those are practical points in favor of the move. What does your gut tell you when you imagine actually being there day to day? Not visiting family, but like, coming home from a shift and being in Cicero?

honestly my gut is confused lol. like some days I think about it and I get excited because I could have dinner with my mom on weeknights and help Sofia with homework and be part of their everyday life instead of just showing up on holidays. but then other days I think about leaving my apartment and my neighborhood and I get this pit in my stomach

It sounds like both reactions are genuine — you're not making one up. The family pull is about connection and belonging, and the Logan Square pull is about independence and your personal identity. Those are both really important things. Have you talked to your parents about it? Do they want you closer, or are they happy with how things are?

my mom would literally throw a parade if I moved to Cicero lol she's been dropping hints for years. but she also doesn't pressure me about it, she just does the classic mom thing where she's like oh mija we had pozole tonight I wish you were here and I'm like MAMA that's emotional warfare

Ha, the pozole guilt trip — that's expert-level mom strategy. But it sounds like she respects your independence even if she wants you closer. What about Marco? Would a move to Cicero affect your relationship at all?

Marco's in Pilsen so it wouldn't be that far from Cicero actually, probably closer than Logan Square. he said he'd support whatever I decide which is sweet but also not helpful because I want someone to just tell me what to do lol

I get that — sometimes you want permission rather than another person saying "it's up to you." But the fact that you're so torn tells me you probably shouldn't rush this. Is there a deadline forcing a decision, like a lease ending?

my lease is up in February so I have a few months to figure it out. I think I'm just going to sit with it for a while and see how I feel after the holidays because being around family during Christmas always either makes me want to move closer or makes me grateful I have my own space to escape to lmao

That's actually a really smart approach — let the holidays be a natural test. Pay attention to how you feel when you're there for extended time versus how you feel when you come back to Logan Square. The answer will probably become clearer once you stop trying to force it. And you've got until February, so there's no need to decide today.

yeah you're right I just need to stop spiraling about it and let it marinate. idk why I'm like this about every big decision I swear I can make split second calls at work when a patient is crashing but ask me to decide where to live and I'm paralyzed

That's because at work there's a clear right answer based on training and protocols. Life decisions don't have that — there's no algorithm for "where should I live to be happy." Give yourself the same compassion you'd give a patient who's struggling with a hard choice. You'll figure it out.
